security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote data could be modified in transit.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not protect vote data integrity during transmission.

assumptions: The network connection between the user and the contract is insecure.
The actor can intercept and modify the vote data before it reaches the contract.

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure communication channels (e.g., HTTPS, TLS).

description: An attacker may intercept and alter the vote before it is processed by the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes are not linked to verifiable identities.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system lacks a mechanism to verify the identity of voters.

assumptions: The system does not employ robust authentication and authorization mechanisms.
The actor can cast votes anonymously.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong user authentication and logging of vote submissions.

description: An attacker may cast votes and deny involvement in the process later.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be exposed through insecure channels.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not protect the confidentiality of votes.

assumptions: The governance contract does not store vote data securely.
The actor can access the contract's database or logs.

mitigation.strategy: Implement encryption and access controls to protect vote data.

description: An attacker may gain access to voting data.
